item.title: Why AI startups are selling the same equity at two different prices
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: Some AI founders are using a novel valuation mechanism to manufacture unicorn status.

item.title: A suite of government hacking tools targeting iPhones is now being used by cybercriminals
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: Security researchers say exploits used by governments to hack into iPhones have been found to be used by cybercriminals. They warned of an emerging market for "secondhand" exploits.

item.title: Alibaba’s Qwen tech lead steps down after major AI push
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: Reactions rippled through Alibaba's Qwen team after tech lead Junyang Lin stepped down following a major model launch.

item.title: Just three companies dominated the $189B in VC investments last month
item.dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis
item.description: Crunchbase data shows a record $189 billion of global venture capital flowed to startups last month, with AI startups nabbing 90% of the capital.

item.title: TikTok down for some in US, thanks to second Oracle outage since sale
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: TikTok experienced a similar outage just days after ByteDance divested the app's U.S. operations.

item.title: AI companies are spending millions to thwart this former tech exec’s congressional bid
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: A tech billionaire-backed super PAC is spending $125 million to undercut candidates pushing for AI regulation. New York's Alex Bores, a former tech executive himself, is one of them.

item.title: ChatGPT’s new GPT-5.3 Instant model will stop telling you to calm down
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: The company says the new model will reduce the "cringe" that's been annoying its users for months.

item.title: Anduril aims at $60 billion valuation in new funding round
item.dc:creator: Russell Brandom
item.description: The funding round would come less than a year after Anduril's Series G, which closed in June with $2.5 billion against a $30 billion valuation.

item.title: Claude Code rolls out a voice mode capability
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: Anthropic is stepping up its game in the AI coding space with the rollout of Voice Mode in Claude Code.

item.title: Android users can now share tracker tag info with airlines to help locate lost luggage
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: More than 10 global airlines now accept Find Hub locations as part of their baggage recovery process.

item.title: X says it will suspend creators from revenue-sharing program for unlabeled AI posts of ‘armed conflict’
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Creators who break the rules will get a three-month suspension, and if they continue to violate the policy, they'll be permanently banned.

item.title: The new MacBook Pro laptops are as much as $400 more expensive than their predecessors. Thank the RAM shortage.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: With the demands for more computers and data centers to power AI, the market is experiencing a shortage of RAM, causing memory prices to surge.

item.title: Hacked traffic cams and hijacked TVs: How cyber operations supported the war against Iran
item.dc:creator: Lorenzo Franceschi-Bicchierai
item.description: After U.S. and Israeli forces started bombing Iran, reports say cyber operations have disrupted communications, supported surveillance activities, and have been used in psychological operations.

item.title: Activist investor Elliott takes a $1B stake in Pinterest, betting on AI-driven growth
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: The news comes as Pinterest has faced serious headwinds. Shares tumbled over the past year, with disappointing earnings, layoffs affecting 15% of its workforce, a declining ad business, and increased rivalry from AI chatbots.

item.title: Apple’s rumored MacBook Neo, a lower-cost, colorful laptop, could launch this week
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: This would mark the first time Apple competes with cheaper hardware like the Chromebook.

item.title: Amid new competition, Chrome speeds up its release schedule
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Google Chrome will begin shipping new releases every two weeks in September, instead of monthly.

item.title: Audible launches a cheaper ‘Standard’ subscription plan, challenging Spotify
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: Under the new Standard plan, subscribers will lose access to the audiobooks they've consumed when they unsubscribe. The Premium plan lets users keep the audiobooks they’ve listened to even if they unsubscribe.

item.title: X begins testing standalone X Chat app on iOS
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: The new app promises a way to send and receive messages without being distracted by your timeline.

item.title: Apple unveils new MacBook Air and MacBook Pro with M5
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: These updated M5 chips were specifically designed to make the MacBook Air and MacBook Pro laptops better at handling intensive AI tasks.

item.title: Apple’s new Studio Displays come with Thunderbolt 5
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: The new displays come with better cameras, new sound systems, and Thunderbolt 5 ports.
